extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection via Jinja2. User-controlled 'name' query parameter is interpolated into an f-string on line 38 to build 'content', which is then passed to Template(content).render(). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., ?name={{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}) to achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code (Code Injection)

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (app/config.py line 11) with no URL validation or allowlist. If an attacker can influence this environment variable (e.g., via container misconfiguration or .env file manipulation), they can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially accessing internal services or metadata endpoints.

Hardcoded credentials and secrets in source code. SUPER_SECRET_NAME (line 13) and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN (line 15) are hardcoded with FIXME comments indicating they should be loaded from environment variables instead. This exposes sensitive values in version control and code repositories.
